questions.questionMd: In a Promise chain such as `fetch(url).then(a).then(b)`, what value does function `b` receive as its argument?

options.text: The original URL string passed to fetch()
The value returned by function a
The HTTP status code of the response
The raw HTTP response object

questions.explanationMd: The return value of a preceding `.then()` block is passed as the argument to the subsequent `.then()` block.

questions.questionMd: In REST API terminology, what is the "base URL"?

options.text: The full URL including all query parameters for a specific request
The fallback URL used when the primary endpoint is unavailable
The root address of the API server to which all endpoint paths are appended
The URL of the web page that consumes the API

questions.explanationMd: The base URL is the common prefix for the routing system of a REST application.

questions.questionMd: To sort an array of album objects by year in ascending order, which comparator function should be passed to `.sort()`?

options.text: function(a, b) { return b.year - a.year; }
function(a, b) { return a.year + b.year; }
function(a, b) { return a.year; }
function(a, b) { return a.year - b.year; }

questions.explanationMd: Subtracting `b.year` from `a.year` yields a negative number if `a` comes before `b`, correctly sorting the array ascendingly.

questions.questionMd: A developer receives the string `'{"name": "Ice Bear"}'` from a server. Which function should they use to turn it into a usable JavaScript object?

options.text: JSON.objectify()
JSON.parse()
JSON.decode()
JSON.stringify()

questions.explanationMd: `JSON.parse()` reads a JSON string and translates it directly to standard JS properties and objects.

questions.questionMd: Compared to a custom plain-text format, what is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JSON files are always smaller in size than plain-text files
JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object, eliminating manual parsing
JSON files do not require a server to be accessed
JSON is the only format supported by the fetch() API

questions.explanationMd: Data mapping and typecasting (like Strings vs Numbers) handle themselves implicitly through `JSON.parse()`.

questions.questionMd: When loading album data from a custom plain-text file, which of the following is a disadvantage mentioned in the lecture?

options.text: Text files do not support arrays of items
Numbers must be manually converted from strings using functions like parseInt()
Plain-text files cannot be served over HTTP
The file cannot be loaded using the fetch() API

questions.explanationMd: Since all content in a plain text file is read as purely textual string data, manual casting functions are needed to parse numbers accurately.

questions.questionMd: Why is the `fetch()` API described as asynchronous in the context of web programming?

options.text: It guarantees that the response arrives faster than synchronous requests
It blocks script execution until the full response is received
It automatically retries failed requests without any extra code
It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response

questions.explanationMd: Asynchronous APIs like fetch are non-blocking, so user interfaces don't freeze while data is traveling back and forth.

questions.questionMd: The lecture recommends implementing the `Album` class before the `App` class. What is the stated reason for this order?

options.text: The fetch() call can only be tested after the Album class exists
The App class depends on the Album class, whereas the Album class has no dependencies
JavaScript requires dependent classes to be declared first in the file
The Album class is simpler and requires fewer lines of code

questions.explanationMd: Good architectural practice typically implements self-contained, dependency-free models (`Album`) before wiring up higher-level coordination logic (`App`).

questions.questionMd: Which of the following best describes a JavaScript Promise?

options.text: An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation
A synchronous function that immediately returns a value
A built-in timer that delays code execution by a specified amount
A special array type used to store multiple fetch results

questions.explanationMd: This is the classic Mozilla Developer Network definition of a Promise.

questions.questionMd: Why does the lecture suggest extracting the album rendering logic into a separate `_renderAlbums()` helper method?

options.text: To avoid exceeding the maximum allowed number of lines in a single method
So the same rendering code can be reused both after the initial fetch and after each sort operation
To prevent the browser from caching the album images between renders
Because the fetch() API requires a dedicated render callback

questions.explanationMd: Extracting rendering logic allows you to re-call it anytime state changes without rewriting code (DRY - Don't Repeat Yourself).

questions.questionMd: When constructing a URL with multiple query parameters, which character is used to separate each parameter after the first one?
questions.explanationMd: The `?` starts the query string and `&` separates additional parameter key-value pairings.

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP method does a browser send automatically whenever you navigate to a URL in the address bar?

options.text: PUT
GET
DELETE
POST

questions.explanationMd: Browser address bar navigation always performs an HTTP GET request to fetch page contents.

questions.questionMd: In the discography page example, how does the `SortButton` class communicate a sort action back to the `App` class?

options.text: It dispatches a custom keyboard event that triggers the App's event listener
It calls an onClickCallback function passed to it during construction, supplying the sort function as an argument
It directly modifies the albumInfo property of the App instance
It fires a built-in DOM click event that the App class listens for on the document

questions.explanationMd: Passing callbacks as constructors props enables decoupled classes to communicate events back up to their parents.

questions.questionMd: Which command is used to serve local files over HTTP so that `fetch()` can access them during development?

options.text: npm start http
node server.js
python3 -m fetch server
python3 -m http.server

questions.explanationMd: Python 3 has a built-in lightweight HTTP server module useful for quick local development testing without CORS/File protocol issues.

questions.questionMd: Which built-in JavaScript array method is used in the lecture to reorder album objects by year?

options.text: .reduce()
.map()
.filter()
.sort()

questions.explanationMd: The `.sort()` method directly handles repositioning array items via a comparator function.
